notes: PREPARATION FOR TRANSFER AT THE JUNIOR LEVEL We encourage Students to take Chinese (Mandarin) language courses if they are offered at their college. Students will receive transfer credit for language courses taken at their community college. However, all UC Berkeley students must take a placement exam for Chinese to ensure enrollment in a course at the correct level for their language skills. Because language courses vary among colleges, a student may need to repeat an introductory or intermediate-level course if necessary. The department undergraduate advisor will work with students to create a program plan to complete their major requirements by their expected graduation date, regardless of which language course is their first. We also offer specific courses for students who are heritage learners. These courses are designated with an X or Y suffix. X is the equivalent of A, and Y is the equivalent of B; for example, the heritage course for the first semester introductory is CHINESE 1Y and it meets the exact requirement as does CHINESE 1A. Please note: Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) exams will not satisfy the requirements for this major. Prerequisites Students are required to successfully (grade C or higher) complete one Chinese Language course at UC Berkeley and Chinese 7A or 7B before declaring the major. More Information The Berkeley Guide and the department website provide more information about major requirements and course information. Advising: Students with questions about transferring into the major in Chinese Language are encouraged to contact our undergraduate major advisor(UMA): Cassandra Dunn Department Website: http://ealc.berkeley.edu/ Email: cassandrajj@berkeley.edu Appointments: Schedule an appointment (in-person or remotely).

COLLEGE ADMISSIONS REQUIREMENTS FOR TRANSFER STUDENTS THIS MAJOR IS OFFERED BY THE COLLEGE OF LETTERS AND SCIENCE (L&S) IMPORTANT information for students applying for admission to L&S for the fall 2025 application cycle and beyond Completion of Essential Skills requirements (Reading & Composition, Quantitative Reasoning, and Foreign Language) or Cal-GETC/IGETC will no longer be a requirement for admission; however, completion of either is strongly recommended prior to enrollment at UC Berkeley to ensure timely degree progress. For more information see the New Policies for Transfer Student Applicants website for L&S: https://ls.berkeley.edu/prospective-students/new-policies-transfer-student-applicants By the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley, you must complete : 1) The 7-course pattern (as found in the UC Transfer Admission Eligibility Courses Menu in ASSIST): · Two transferable courses in English composition; · One transferable course in mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning; · Four transferable college courses chosen from at least two of the following subject areas: o arts and humanities o social and behavioral sciences o physical and biological sciences AND 2) Major Requirements : Follow the guidance on preparation for this major below. For more information on admission to UC Berkeley : http://admissions.berkeley.edu For specific information on transfer admission to UC Berkeley: https://admissions.berkeley.edu/apply-to-berkeley/transfer-students/transfer-requirements/ For more information on majors at UC Berkeley : Berkeley Academic Guide: http://guide.berkeley.edu
